document_map.document_title: EXHIBIT D — DUTY OF CARE & TRAVEL RISK MANAGEMENT SERVICES
document_map.document_purpose_summary: This Exhibit defines Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services provided by Direct Travel to support Client's traveler protection program, including severity level classifications, service level objectives, data handling requirements, and applicable limitations.

sections.section_purpose: Define scope, regulatory context, and standard of care
sections.section_summary: Establishes the purpose of the Exhibit, identifies applicable regulatory frameworks for duty of care obligations under OSHA, UK, and EU law, and defines the standard of care Direct Travel will apply to service delivery.
sections.beginning_text: This Exhibit defines (a) Duty of Care services Direct Travel will provide to support Client's travel risk management ("TRM") program, (b) Severity Level classifications and service level objectives, (c) roles and responsibilities, (d) data handling and privacy requirements, and (e) limitations applicable to such services.

sections.section_name: SEVERITY LEVELS AND SERVICE LEVEL OBJECTIVES
sections.section_purpose: Define incident severity tiers and corresponding SLO timings
sections.section_summary: Defines four severity levels (Advisory through Crisis) with corresponding traveler alert timings, client escalation procedures, status update frequencies, traveler outreach response protocols, and exceptions to SLO compliance.
sections.beginning_text:
| Level | Classification | Description | Examples |
| **Level 1** | Advisory | Minor disruption; informational alert only | Localized weather warning, minor transport delay |
| **Level 2** | Monitor | Moderate disruption; targeted alerts; optional welfare check | Regional transport strike, elevated security alert |
| **Level 3** | Action Required | Significant disruption; proactive outreach required | Major weather event in Traveler area, airport closure, disease outbreak |

sections.section_purpose: Govern data handling, privacy obligations, and cross-border transfers
sections.section_summary: Establishes data controller and processor roles, defines lawful basis for processing under GDPR, specifies minimum required traveler profile data elements, constrains data sharing to authorized parties and purposes, and addresses location data and cross-border transfer safeguards.
sections.beginning_text: Client is the **data controller** for Traveler Data. Direct Travel acts as a **data processor**, processing Traveler Data solely on Client's documented instructions. Where required, a Data Processing Agreement ("**DPA**") is incorporated by reference. Processing is supported by Client's **legitimate interest** in protecting health and safety of travelers under Article 6(1)(f) GDPR and equivalent laws.

global_entity_pre_registration.brief_description: The organization contracting for Direct Travel's services; referred to as 'Client' throughout the document; first appears in the document header as the counterparty to the Travel Services Agreement and data controller for Traveler Data.

Travel management company providing Duty of Care and Travel Risk Management services; referred to as 'Direct Travel, Inc.,' 'Direct Travel,' and 'TMC' (Travel Management Company); appears in document header as service provider and data processor.

Individual traveling on behalf of Client; the subject of welfare checks, status monitoring, and data protection under this Exhibit; referred to as 'Traveler' in section headings and outreach standards.

Information about travelers including names, contact numbers, email addresses, emergency contacts, passport details, and location information; introduced in SEC-01 Purpose and defined comprehensively in SEC-03 with minimum required data elements.

Advisory severity level for minor disruptions requiring informational alerts only; defined in SEC-02.1 Severity Level Definitions with example incidents such as localized weather warnings and minor transport delays.

Monitor severity level for moderate disruptions requiring targeted alerts and optional welfare checks; defined in SEC-02.1 Severity Level Definitions with examples including regional transport strikes and elevated security alerts.

Action Required severity level for significant disruptions requiring proactive outreach to affected travelers; defined in SEC-02.1 Severity Level Definitions with examples such as major weather events, airport closures, and disease outbreaks.

Crisis severity level for immediate life safety risks requiring urgent outreach and crisis escalation; defined in SEC-02.1 Severity Level Definitions with examples including terrorist attacks, catastrophic natural disasters, and pandemic border closures.

cross_section_dependencies.dependency_description: SEC-02.3 identifies 'incomplete Traveler profile data' as an SLO exception factor, and SEC-03.3 defines the minimum required Traveler profile data elements (name, mobile phone, email, emergency contact, passport details) necessary for understanding the scope of this SLO impact.

SEC-02.4 prescribes welfare check channels (SMS, email, push notification, voice call) whose effectiveness and applicability are conditionally dependent on availability of specific Traveler Data elements (mobile phone for SMS, email address for email) defined in SEC-03.3.

SEC-02.4 defines TMC outreach actions and response procedures; SEC-03.4 modifies and constrains those actions by restricting data sharing to authorized recipients and purposes, thereby limiting what information can be shared during outreach-related activities.
